comments.text: Colour $n$ red if $v_2(n)$ is even and colour $n$ blue if $v_2(n)$ is odd. Then $n$ and $2n$ are different colours for all $n\in\mathbb{N}$.
But $n$ and $2n$ need not both be in $A+A$. In this colouring, for example, one could take $A$ to be the set of all integers $\equiv 1\pmod{4}$ - then all elements of $A+A$ are blue.
